mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a robust and expanding economy. This leads to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest PAYEMS reading shows continued job growth post-2008 crisis.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 6-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, monetary policy is maximally accommodative. This environment suppresses risk-free rates, encourages risk-taking, and supports higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been held in the 0-0.25% range since late 2008.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05) during periods when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in retail sales (RSAFS) and an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a sharp contraction in consumer health. This joint signal precedes negative earnings revisions and leads to negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0% AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is the primary driver of the US economy.
failure_condition: In instances where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 10-year lookback period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals that supply chains are normalizing and manufacturing activity is recovering. This leads to positive earnings surprises and boosts equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The economy is in a sustained recovery phase following the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 12-month change in INDPRO and 60-day forward returns is negative during the 24 months following an NBER-declared recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a key psychological and economic level (e.g. 5.5%), it signals the economy is approaching full employment. This boosts consumer confidence and spending, providing a tailwind for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The latest unemployment rate is 5.3%, a post-crisis low.
failure_condition: The median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not higher when UNRATE is below 5.5% compared to when it is above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) while the labor market is already tight (low UNRATE) creates a hawkish dilemma for the Federal Reserve. Markets price in a higher probability of monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has a dual mandate of price stability and maximum employment.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change in housing starts (HOUST) is a high-frequency leading indicator of broad economic activity and big-ticket consumer spending. Positive momentum in HOUST signals strong underlying demand, positively impacting equity markets over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a key interest-rate sensitive sector with large multiplier effects.
failure_condition: The 3-month rolling correlation between the 3-month change in HOUST and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth is the broadest measure of economic health. A higher-than-average rate of change signals strong corporate revenue growth, leading to positive revisions of earnings expectations and supporting higher equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The latest GDPC1 release confirmed continued economic expansion.
failure_condition: The information coefficient between quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically greater than zero over the full sample period.
